Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Oleg,

This is fine, but we have to be a little careful about this patch - if a client where to be built with this patch and without the "limit grant requests to < 2 GiB" patch we merged recently, it would break grant.

Just an FYI.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

The patch should be functional, but it would be better to push a new version.

(1 comment)

Patch Set 4:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 4:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 4:

Remember as we look at this:
Maximum grant to one client per OSC is 2 GiB.  So dirty_mb being higher than that won't change anything except whether we wait for dirty_mb or grant.

Patch Set 4: -Code-Review

I won't stand in your way if you want to land this patch as is.

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2

Patch Set 4:

Let's get this landed early to ensure we have enough testing on larger and smaller systems.

I'm a bit concerned that our writeback code doesn't handle memory pressure very well, but we'll see that when this patch is landed by hang/OOM test failures.

Patch Set 4:

> I'm a bit concerned that our writeback code doesn't handle memory
 > pressure very well, but we'll see that when this patch is landed by
 > hang/OOM test failures.

Hopefully grant will take care of it. We certainyl will need to shift more of that logic into grants anyway as we are proceeding max dirty.

Also don't forget we have system-wide cached (and that includes dirty too) limit at 1/2 RAM too, so we cannot get too big in here anyway.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Removed the following votes:

* Code-Review-1 by HPDD Checkpatch (377)
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 92e2b514e06ca2cd8c83e7439e206a629ba508f2 by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-10986 lfs: make lfs project tolerant errors

commit.subject: LU-10990 osc: increase default max_dirty_mb to 2G
commit.message:
LU-10990 osc: increase default max_dirty_mb to 2G

While ideally we want to go away from max_dirty_mb setting
completely and let grants code to take the msot part of it,
Andreas raises a somewhat valid point that for certain
system configurations with high-latency links, system
administrators might want to have ability to limit
amount of dirty pages just for those OSCs to limit amount
of time it might take to flush that dirty data.

So a good compromise is to lift the max_dirty_mb default
value first while we work out the current grant code
deficiencies

Change-Id: I4de407088af70e0f98f0563160217ba70a635dfb
Signed-off-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/32288
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Patrick Farrell <paf@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
